subject: Product Management
title: Multivariate Testing
description: A quick, practical guide to Multivariate Testing.

hook.text: An A/B test changes one thing. A multivariate test changes multiple things at once and finds the best combination.

buildup.text: Test a headline (3 options) × button color (3 options) × image (3 options) simultaneously. That's 27 combinations in one test.

discovery.text: Multivariate testing reveals interaction effects: maybe the red button works best with headline A but worst with headline C.

twist.text: The catch: 27 combinations need 27× more traffic to reach significance.

climax.text: Use A/B tests for most experiments. Reserve multivariate testing for high-traffic pages where combinations matter.

punchline.text: A/B tests for most decisions. Multivariate for high-traffic combinations.

quiz.question: What is the main limitation of multivariate testing?
options: It requires much more traffic to reach significance — most products don't have enough users
Multivariate tests are always more accurate
They can only test two variables at once
